content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be restructured to prioritize "curiosity relics"—partial, unresolved memory traces of unanticipated exploration, rather than just storing fully labeled high-value training samples. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where unresolved curiosity traces in long-term memory actively drive new active learning query selection, rather than query selection being determined solely by current model uncertainty.
